method_atom: a threshold instability in a disordered amplifying medium in which the same constituent elements supply both amplification (gain) and multiplicative feedback via random multiple scattering, producing a sharp onset of self-sustained coherent output once a single dimensionless control parameter (the product of density and per-element interaction strength) crosses a critical value; the threshold, output intensity, and output frequency are predicted quantitatively using transport models of energy propagation through the random gain medium together with the saturable response of the individual amplifying elements
object_atom: a cloud of cold atoms acting simultaneously as gain and scattering medium for light, characterized by the on-resonance optical thickness b0 and the polarizability of strongly-pumped two-level atoms, emitting laser light above a critical b0 on the order of 300
brief_reason: the gain-plus-random-scattering feedback and single-critical-parameter threshold machinery is transferable across wave/amplification systems, while the cold-atom cloud providing both gain and scattering with optical thickness b0 is the atomic-optics-specific object.
